title: [cmake] Remove CIRCT_SLANG_BUILD_FROM_SOURCE flag?
body:
CIRCT_SLANG_BUILD_FROM_SOURCE=Off allows users to build with installed slang but we are also using a specific commit of slang:
https://github.com/llvm/circt/blob/304b3b7485fec6cd387026ecbfd316c28f5f6cfe/CMakeLists.txt#L568


However when CIRCT_SLANG_BUILD_FROM_SOURCE=Off slang will be set to v9.0: 
https://github.com/llvm/circt/blob/304b3b7485fec6cd387026ecbfd316c28f5f6cfe/CMakeLists.txt#L614-L615

This may still compile but tests won't pass regardless. 
Certainly we will continue using a specific commit so not sure CIRCT_SLANG_BUILD_FROM_SOURCE is meaningful anymore.
